{"command":"Import-Module PnP.PowerShell; Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Detailed | Out-String -Width 220","explanation":"Read local help for the PnP Entra ID app registration helper to identify required parameters.","goal":"Inspect app registration command","mode":"sync","timeout":120000}

NAME
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers an Entra ID App for use with Interactive login
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName <String> [-AzureEnvironment <AzureEnvironment>] [-DeviceLogin <SwitchParameter>] [-EntraIDLoginEndPoint <String>] [-LogoFilePath <String>] 
    [-MicrosoftGraphEndPoint <String>] -Tenant <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName <String> [-AzureEnvironment <AzureEnvironment>] [-DeviceLogin <SwitchParameter>] [-EntraIDLoginEndPoint <String>] [-LogoFilePath <String>] 
    [-MicrosoftGraphEndPoint <String>] -Tenant <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers an Entra ID App for use with the interactive login on Connect-PnPOnline. By default it assumes an Interactive login, but you can decide to use Device Login auth by specifying -DeviceLogin.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ApplicationName <String>
        The name of the Entra ID Application to create.
        
    -AzureEnvironment [<AzureEnvironment>]
        The Azure environment to use for authentication, the defaults to 'Production' which is the main Azure environment.
        
    -DeviceLogin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If specified, a device login flow, supporting Multi-Factor Authentication will be used to authenticate towards the Microsoft Graph.
        
    -EntraIDLoginEndPoint [<String>]
        Sets the EntraID login endpoint to be used for creation of the app. This only works if Azure Environment parameter is set to `Custom`
        
    -GraphApplicationPermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft Graph Application permissions to request.
        
    -GraphDelegatePermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft Graph Delegate permissions to request.
        
    -LogoFilePath [<String>]
        Sets the logo for the Entra ID application. Provide a full path to a local image file on your disk which you want to use as the logo.
        
    -MicrosoftGraphEndPoint [<String>]
        Sets the Microsoft Graph endpoint to be used for creation of the app. This only works if Azure Environment parameter is set to `Custom`
        
    -SharePointApplicationPermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft SharePoint Application permissions to request.
        
    -SharePointDelegatePermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft SharePoint Delegate permissions to request.
        
    -SignInAudience [<String>]
        Sets the sign in audience. Use this to make the app support Single tenant accounts, Multi-tenant accounts, Multi-tenant + personal accounts & personal accounts only.
        
    -Tenant <String>
        The identifier of your tenant, e.g. mytenant.onmicrosoft.com
        
    <CommonParameters>
        This cmdlet supports the common parameters: Verbose, Debug,
        ErrorAction, ErrorVariable, WarningAction, WarningVariable,
        OutBuffer, PipelineVariable, and OutVariable. For more information, see
        about_CommonParameters (https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=113216). 
    
    --------- EXAMPLE 1 ---------
    
    ```powershell
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName TestApp -Tenant yourtenant.onmicrosoft.com
    ```
    Creates a new Entra ID Application registration. The application will be setup with the following delegate permissions to consent: AllSites.FullControl, Group.ReadWrite.All, User.ReadWrite.All, 
    TermStore.ReadWrite.All. A browser window will be shown allowing you to authenticate.
    
     --------- EXAMPLE 2 ---------
    
    ```powershell
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName TestApp -Tenant yourtenant.onmicrosoft.com -GraphDelegatePermissions "Group.Read.All" -SharePointDelegatePermissions "AllSites.FullControl"
    ```
    Creates a new Entra ID Application registration. The application will be setup with the following delegate permissions to consent: Group.Read.All, AllSites.FullControl. A browser window will be shown allowing you to 
    authenticate.
    
 REMARKS
    To see the examples, type: "Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Examples"
    For more information, type: "Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Detailed"
    For technical information, type: "Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Full"
    For online help, type: "Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Online"
